access.user: Can upload in the comment attachment flow; download permissions must be re-validated.
access.admin: No standalone public 'upload management console' currently exists.
access.agent: No upload capability is currently provided.

rules.comment-attachments-only: Uploads serve only comment attachments; they are not an independent file space.
rules.permission-and-quota: Permission and quota checks are required before upload.
rules.three-step-upload: Web comment attachment uploads follow a three-step flow — create upload session on-site → browser direct upload to S3-compatible pre-signed URL generated by AWS SDK → confirm completion on-site; page CSP must allow the signed storage origin actually used by the upload backend, including AWS S3 virtual-hosted / path-style addresses or compatible backend addresses via custom endpoint configuration.
rules.s3-compatible-backend: The storage backend operates with AWS SDK S3-compatible configuration; defaults to AWS S3 but allows connecting to compatible backends via custom endpoint.
rules.e2e-local-s3: E2E tests use a local S3-compatible test endpoint by default; external storage is only used when an available S3 environment is explicitly provided.
rules.download-auth-required: Downloads must not bypass authorization by obtaining a direct link.
rules.download-signed-url: /api/uploads/[id]/download only returns a short-lived signed download URL redirect, not a stable long-reusable object direct link.

notes: The same path currently handles both 'read upload quota / history' and 'create upload session' capabilities.

routes.returns: { key: String, url: String, maxFileSizeBytes: Int, quotaBytes: Int, usedBytes: Int }
{ upload: Upload, usedBytes: Int, quotaBytes: Int }

notes: Validates access permissions before redirecting to a short-lived signed URL.
Renames one upload owned by the current user.
Deletes one upload owned by the current user and removes the object from storage.
